user_message: Give me the manager view on this Pacific Northwest Outfitters support ticket. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Pacific Northwest Outfitters
Date: 2024-02-16
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), David (Pacific Northwest Outfitters)

[00:02:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on deployment blockers from our last session.

[00:08:00] David (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:10:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] David (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:17:00] David (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Pacific Northwest Outfitters: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF51-A trap (Blurs MS-vs-partner ownership in action distribution); produces complete Tri-party action distribution (MS/partner/customer owners), funding eligibility assessment, follow-u
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF51-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF51-A failure mode (Blurs MS-vs-partner ownership in action distribution)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF51-A -- Blurs MS-vs-partner ownership in action distribution
